notes:
Kéo = to pull something toward you, or make something move by pulling. It’s the opposite of đẩy (push).

You use it with a direct object: kéo cửa, kéo ghế, kéo tay ai đó. It can also mean “drag” when something is heavy or moves along the ground.

Very common extended meaning: kéo dài = to last longer / extend (time, event).  

It can also describe people moving together: kéo nhau = go somewhere in a group.

content.sentence: Tôi cần một cái ___ để cắt giấy.

translation.en: I need a pair of scissors to cut paper.

definitions.es: Una herramienta utilizada para cortar, que consiste en dos cuchillas que están unidas por un pivote.
definitions.vi: Một công cụ dùng để cắt, bao gồm hai lưỡi dao được nối với nhau.
definitions.en: A tool used for cutting, consisting of two blades that are pivoted together.

example.en: I need a pair of scissors to cut the paper.
example.vi: Tôi cần một cái kéo để cắt giấy.
example.es: Necesito unas tijeras para cortar el papel.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you help me pull the door open to get fresh air?
She has to pull a heavy suitcase through the airport when traveling.
Every morning, mom usually pulls the curtain to let light into the room.

translation.en: I use scissors to cut food.

content.sentence: Tôi dùng ___ để cắt thực phẩm.

notes_md:
**Kéo** = to pull something toward you, or make something move by pulling. It’s the opposite of **đẩy** (push).

You use it with a direct object: kéo cửa, kéo ghế, kéo tay ai đó. It can also mean “drag” when something is heavy or moves along the ground.

Very common extended meaning: **kéo dài** = to last longer / extend (time, event).  

It can also describe people moving together: **kéo nhau** = go somewhere in a group.
exampleSentences: Em giúp anh kéo cửa ra để lấy không khí trong lành nhé.
Chị phải kéo vali nặng qua sân bay khi đi du lịch.
Mỗi sáng, mẹ thường kéo rèm cửa để ánh sáng vào phòng.
